title: centaurea × toroczkoeensis
intro: Centaurea × toroczkoeensis, also known as Torok's knapweed or Toroczkoe's knapweed, is a hybrid plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, derived from the cross between Centaurea cyanus and Centaurea scabiosa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Centaurea × toroczkoeensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be used in teas, tinctures, or infusions, allowing for the extraction of its medicinal compounds and utilization of its health-promoting properties.
